last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

lines: Context : hr: {i : int, c : byte list, k : key, n : nonce, z : block,
(gsv = OCC.gs /\ kv = k /\ nv = n /\ iv = i) /\
c ++ gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes take_xor (cc gs0) k0 n0 i p =
gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes take_xor (cc gs0) k0 n0 1 p0) /\
(k, n, C.ofintd i).`3 = C.ofintd iv /\ OCC.gs = gsv) &&
result = cc gsv kv nv (C.ofintd iv) =>
let p1 = drop block_size p in
gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes take_xor (cc gs0) k0 n0 (i + 1) p1 =
gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes take_xor (cc gs0) k0 n0 1 p0) /\

moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

checks: Keep one-sided instrumentation as a map, not as a tactic recipe.

obligation_families.effect: The pure goal exposes tuple/record projections or constructor equalities that can connect program variables to map/list keys.
obligation_families.limitations: Projection equalities are reported only when visible in the current text.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

evidence: pure_tail_surface is visible for the current goal
current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual
limitations: does not select a destructor, witness, rewrite, or SMT lemma
reported checkpoints are recovery references, not a default route

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: No fresh recommendation is available; parse the current goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: The current procedure frontier exposes a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, while, seq, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid one-sided hoare/phoare loop form.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

items.label: After call opened / before obligation work #11
items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #11 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before call invariant #10
items.committed_tactic: call (_: k = kv /\ n = nv /\ c = C.ofintd iv /\ OCC.gs = gsv ==> res = cc gsv kv nv (C.ofintd iv)).
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #10 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Return to resume start
items.why_checkpoint: resume boundary; selecting it preserves the verified replay prefix and removes tactics committed after resume
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #7 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
